title: Irish Beef Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful beef stew originating from Ireland, perfect for a cold day. This recipe is a classic take on a comforting dish.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the beef and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the beef from the pot and set it aside.
Add the onions to the pot and cook until they start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the carrots and potatoes to the pot and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the red wine to the pot and cook until it has reduced by half, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Add the beef broth, browned beef, and Irish stew seasoning to the pot.
Bring the stew to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the oven.
Bake the stew for 2 1/2 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh parsley if desired.
